paper.title: Analysis of The Anomalous Orbital-Energy Changes Observed in Spacecraft Flybys of Earth

anomaly.name: Earth Flyby Anomaly
anomaly.description:
Unaccounted for changes in spacecraft speed, both increases and decreases, observed during
Earth flybys of six different spacecraft from December 8, 1990 to August 2, 2005. The anomaly
appears as a small, centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration toward Earth, similar
to the Pioneer anomaly. All potential sources of systematic error have been modeled and cannot
account for the observed anomalies.

assumption.statement: The Earth flyby anomaly is caused by a small acceleration (in addition to natural gravitation), centrally directed and independent of distance, directed toward the core center of Earth
assumption.rationale: Ellman proposes that the flyby anomaly shares the same characteristics as the Pioneer anomaly - a centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration

assumption.statement: The Flyby Anomaly and Pioneer Anomaly share the same cause - a centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration
assumption.rationale: Ellman argues that both anomalies exhibit the same characteristics and thus likely have the same underlying cause

assumption.statement: A universal decay of the length dimension of all material reality causes the anomalous accelerations observed in flybys, Pioneer spacecraft, and galactic rotation curves
assumption.rationale: Ellman proposes that a universal length decay would produce centrally directed, distance-independent accelerations without conflicting with planetary ephemeris

claim.statement: Six different spacecraft involved in Earth flybys from December 8, 1990 to August 2, 2005 showed unaccounted for changes in spacecraft speed, both increases and decreases
The magnitude of the anomalous acceleration component parallel to the spacecraft flight path depends on latitude, with the effective acceleration being A(λ) = A·Cos[λ], where λ is the latitude

Depending on the specific flight path, a spacecraft may experience overall net anomalous acceleration or deceleration, or zero net modification if the path is perfectly symmetrical relative to the equator

The same anomalous centrally directed, distance-independent acceleration occurs relative to Earth (flyby anomaly), relative to the Sun (Pioneer anomaly), in galactic rotation curves, and in rotating groups of galaxies
A universal decay of the length dimension would not conflict with the planetary ephemeris and would not be detectable except in unusual circumstances like the Pioneer and Flyby anomalies

prediction.statement: Experiments to test the universal decay and measure its parameters are proposed to verify the theory
